scenario_title: delete_rrset on a non-existent type after passing prereqs
test_rationale: This test first ensures the UPDATE is otherwise admissible by asserting an existing A rrset at the owner name, then issues a delete_rrset for MX even though no MX RRset exists at that node. That isolates the underspecified choice point in RFC 2136: whether absent-type deletion is silently successful or reported as an error. The follow-up MX query records the post-state, which should remain absent in either case, so the primary oracle is the UPDATE reply RCODE.

group_1.behavior: Treats delete_rrset for an absent MX rrset as a successful no-op after prerequisites pass.

group_2.behavior: Rejects the UPDATE because the targeted RRset does not exist, returning a semantic failure.

expected_divergence.why_divergence: RFC 2136 §3.4.2.2 defines the delete_rrset syntax, but RFC 2136 §3.4.1 does not require a specific RCODE when the target RRset is absent and all prerequisites succeed. As a result, compliant servers may either accept the UPDATE as an idempotent no-op or fail it with NXRRSET/SERVFAIL-like semantics, while the visible post-state can remain unchanged.
